grants.title: k4 — open family mesh protocol specification
grants.notes: Specification writing grant. k4-agent-hub is live; spec needed so others can implement compatible hubs without our infra. Operator must rewrite 'I built this because I needed it' paragraph in own voice. EU eligibility unconfirmed — verify with NLnet before submit.

grants.title: Phoenix Navigator (Node Zero) beta hardware + accessibility manual
grants.notes: Hardware beta distribution grant. Operator is AuDHD + hypoparathyroidism — lived-experience narrative is the core of the application. 500-word narrative must be written in operator's voice (no ghostwriting). Portal opens May 15 — submit day one.
requirements: proof of autism diagnosis (optional but strengthens)

grants.title: Node One (Totem) prototype + IP protection + DOBE cert
grants.notes: Explicitly funds IP filings and hardware for neurodivergent builders. Strong fit: Node One is assistive tech for somatic regulation, built by an AuDHD operator. Reopens June 1 — apply same day.

grants.title: Cognitive Passport AI augmentation layer
grants.notes: 75K grant for AI augmentation of Cognitive Passport (multi-modal capture: speech-to-text, vision-to-text, reasoning-over-context). Requires 501(c)(3) determination letter but pending status with CP575E is typically accepted. Wait for IRS determination before submitting to be safe.

grants.title: Physical layer completion for sentinel system
grants.notes: Applied April 2026. Decision by ~May 15. If awarded, funds will purchase Home Assistant host ($55), Bangle.js 2 ($63), and two kids tablets ($200 each) — sentinel physical layer enabling LAN HA + biometric feed + mesh device identity.

grants.title: NIDILRR Switzer Fellowship (early-career research training)
grants.funder: National Institute on Disability, Independent Living, and Rehabilitation Research
grants.notes: FY2027 cycle (applications open ~Fall 2026). Requires university partnership as host institution. Potential host: Georgia Tech Center for Assistive Technology. Investigate eligibility and partnership pathway.

grants.title: Field Initiated Projects (FIP) — three-year research program
grants.notes: Major federal disability research grant (~$750K over 3 years). Requires university partnership as prime applicant with P31 Labs as sub-awardee. Investigate sub-contract pathways through Georgia Tech or UGA.
requirements: substantial matching funds or in-kind contribution

archived.title: ESG Housing Grant
archived.notes: Draft existed but was not submitted. Deadline passed. Remove from active pipeline.

archived.title: Gates Grand Challenges — AI to Accelerate Charitable Giving
archived.notes: Draft received but required 40% budget cut + citation verification. Deadline passed before edits completed. Likely closed for this cycle. Archive and prepare for next annual cycle.

archived.title: Shuttleworth Foundation Fellowship
archived.notes: Shuttleworth Fellowship program closed permanently in 2024. Removed from pipeline.

archived.title: Simons Foundation Autism Research Initiative
archived.notes: Requires 501(c)(3) determination before application. Not eligible until IRS determination received.

recommendedImmediateActions: Submit NLnet NGI Zero Commons (€15K) before June 1 — operator voice review is the ONLY blocker
Write ASAN 500-word narrative (opens May 15) — due within 14 days of portal open
Deploy p31-cortex Worker if not already live; verify /api/grant/run returns seeded data
Fix grant-radar.yml syntax error (already done in this session)
Submit Stimpunks June 1 — content pack ready, just submit when portal opens
Monitor Awesome Foundation decision (due ~May 15) — prepare to allocate funds if awarded
